goal: Find CLM nn_core Tensor FFI->BLAS crossover + measure best MFU vs Mac M3 156.7 GF/s Accelerate ceiling across real CLM shapes (d=16..768, seq=8..32, nl=2..8)
ceiling_source: Accelerate sgemm 512^3 f32 measured in #25 ceiling analysis (commit e48b021f)

regime_classification.blas_bound: achieved_gflops > 30 GF/s (BLAS throughput is the bottleneck)
regime_classification.memory_bound: ms * d * seq > 10 ms (activation alloc/free churn)
regime_classification.mixed: none of the above — all shapes tested landed here

crossover_note: NO shape crossed the 30 GF/s BLAS-bound threshold. Peak achieved 2.59 GF/s at d=768 seq=16 — 11.6x below threshold, 58x below ceiling. FFI->BLAS crossover does NOT exist in current Tensor port: the inner scalar loops (rms_norm, softmax, swiglu, attention element-wise) dominate total step time, denying BLAS the opportunity to become the bottleneck.

monotonicity.gflops_grows_with_seq: partial — saturates above seq=16 at small d
monotonicity.observation: GF/s rises sub-linearly with D because only matmul FLOPs scale with D^2 while scalar-loop overhead scales with D (rmsnorm, swiglu, ffn activation) or D*S (softmax, attention scores). matmul share of total FLOPs grows slowly with D, so achievable MFU also grows slowly.

blockers.name: hexa native codegen uses boxed int arithmetic in inner loops
blockers.evidence: macOS `sample` on PID 5248 showed 90%+ of CPU time in layer_bwd_t->attn_bwd_t->hexa_mul/hexa_add, which are boxed-integer runtime functions. Index computations like (i*D+p)*4 go through HexaVal boxing rather than native C int ops.
blockers.lever: emit native int arithmetic in hexa_v2 codegen for integer index expressions inside while bodies

blockers.name: Per-element scalar loops dominate vs BLAS time share
blockers.evidence: Even at d=768 seq=16 where matmul is 4x larger than elementwise, total ms=532 while estimated BLAS time < 5ms. 98%+ of time is in scalar rms_norm/softmax/swiglu/attention/cross-entropy loops.
blockers.expected_uplift: 50x if scalar loops are vectorised or elided
blockers.lever: write tensor-level SIMD kernels for rmsnorm/softmax/swiglu via BLAS/LAPACK primitives (e.g. vDSP_vsmul + vDSP_maxv + cblas_sscal) or inline ARM NEON intrinsics in hexa codegen

blockers.name: attn_fwd + attn_bwd attention inner loops O(S^2*D) scalar
blockers.evidence: attn_fwd_t and attn_bwd_t compute QK^T, softmax, PV, and grads via per-element nested loops instead of BLAS sgemm on [S,S] = [S,D]@[D,S]. The sample showed ~60% of run_shape time was inside attn_bwd_t for the killed shape.
blockers.lever: rewrite attn_fwd/attn_bwd to use t_matmul/t_matmul_transB for Q@K^T and probs@V, keeping only softmax scalar (which should also move to a vectorised primitive)

blockers.name: PBV list growth per layer (minor)
blockers.evidence: layer_fwds.push, layer_inputs.push, dys.push are PBV list-copy operations (feedback_hexa_lists_pbv). For nl=12 this is O(L^2) struct copies per step. Not the main bottleneck at nl<=8 but would matter at real CLM depth.
blockers.lever: pre-allocate fixed-length arrays or use primitive [] with known capacity

next_actions: Rewrite attn_fwd_t / attn_bwd_t to use t_matmul for the 4 big matmuls (Q@K^T, softmax@V, dctx@V^T, dctx@P) — expected 10-50x on attention-heavy shapes
Emit native C int arithmetic in hexa codegen for index expressions — expected 5-20x on all scalar inner loops
Add cblas_sgemv-based vectorised rms_norm and softmax primitives to self/ml/tensor.hexa
Rerun sweep — expected best MFU to jump from 1.65% to 20%+ (CLOSE threshold)

infra_notes.platform: darwin arm64 (Mac M3 base, 24 GB unified)

prior_tensor_bench_tiny.honest_caveat_from_prior_agent: tiny shape is FFI-bound (~50K flops/step), not BLAS-bound; the 1479x is from killing list-append O(N^2) churn, not BLAS throughput
